body.title: Hide IMAP from all public-facing agent APIs
body.result: DatacenterClient now takes flat identity args; bus/connection.py factory hides IMAPServer from announce/; 28 call sites updated; README fixed; listener/invalidator/channels deferred to follow-up
body.description:
IMAP is a bus transport detail. No agent-facing API should ever expose IMAPServer or require callers to instantiate or pass one. Currently DatacenterClient accepts imap_server= as a constructor parameter and the README 'plug in an agent' example shows it. Both are wrong.

Fix: DatacenterClient manages its own IMAP connection internally — callers pass only agent identity (agent_id, instance, surfaces). Remove imap_server= from the constructor. Audit all other public-facing surfaces (announce/, bus/ public API) for any other IMAP leakage.

Fix README 'Plug in an agent' example to show only:
    client = DatacenterClient(agent_id='my-agent', instance='my-agent-0001')
    client.announce()

**Affected files:** unseen_university/announce/client.py (or equivalent DatacenterClient), README.md
**Design rules:** bus/ owns comms:// routing — nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly
**Scope boundary:** IN — remove imap_server from DatacenterClient constructor; fix README example; audit announce/ public API for IMAP leakage; OUT — internal bus/ refactoring
**Completion criteria:** grep -r 'IMAPServer' announce/ returns 0 results; DatacenterClient constructor takes no transport arguments; README example contains no IMAP references
